negative_cases.why_wrong: Rdist Erlang Latency: forces the main output to zero, so coarse compilation passes but behavioral stimulus points fail.
Rdist Erlang Latency: uses a shifted or inverted decision condition that can pass one mode but fails boundary cases.
Rdist Erlang Latency: drops the reported random metric after computing the output, breaking the output/metric consistency check.
Rdist Erlang Latency: keeps the main datapath plausible while reporting the wrong metric value.
Rdist Erlang Latency: uses a wrong gain or scale factor, leaving shape plausible while calibrated checks fail.
